entries.title: COMMENTARY ON PAUL'S EPISTLES on romans 1:6
entries.excerpt: This is by the mission of us who are preaching the name of Christ to all the nations, among whom you too have been called, because the gift of God has been sent to all, so that when they hear that they have been called along with others, they will know that they must not act as if they are under the law, since the other nations accepted the faith of Christ without the law of Moses.

entries.title: RUDIMENTARY EXPOSITION OF THE EPISTLE TO THE ROMANS 6 on romans 1:6
entries.excerpt: Paul teaches here that this salvation had come not only to the Jews, as some Jewish Christians thought.

entries.title: Homily on Romans 1 on romans 1:6
entries.excerpt:
"Among whom are ye also the called of Jesus Christ."

That is, along with whom ye also are: and he does not say, that he called the others with you, but you with the others. For if in Christ Jesus there is neither bond nor free, much less is there king and private man. For even ye were called and did not come over of yourselves.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE EPISTLE TO THE ROMANS.116 on romans 1:6
entries.excerpt: Paul is said to be called to be an apostle, and the Romans are also called, though not to be apostles. Rather they are called to be holy in the obedience of faith. We have already spoken above about the different callings.

entries.title: INTERPRETATION OF THE LETTER TO THE ROMANS on romans 1:6
entries.excerpt: Paul tells them here that he is not doing anything improper, nor invading fields assigned to others, for God had appointed him to preach to the Gentiles.

entries.title: Commentary on Romans on romans 1:6
entries.excerpt: Here he crushes the arrogance of the Romans. You have not received more than the other nations, over whom you consider yourselves masters; therefore, just as we preach to the other nations, so also to you: do not boast. Otherwise: you too were called, you were preceded by grace, and did not come on your own.

entries.title: Commentary on Romans on romans 1:6
entries.excerpt:
Fourth, as to his power over those to whom he was writing and who were subject to his apostleship. Hence he says among whom, i.e., I number among those subject to my apostolate even you Romans howsoever lofty: he lays it low, the lofty city, the foot of the poor, i.e., of Christ, tramples it, the steps of the needy, namely, of the apostles Peter and Paul (Isa 26:5ff.); we were the first to come all the way to you with the Gospel of Christ (2 Cor 10:14).

He adds the called of Jesus Christ, in accord with Hosea: I will call 'not my people' mine (Hos 1:9); or, you are called that you may be of Jesus Christ, as is said below: those whom he predestined he also called (Rom 8:30). Or, you are called of Jesus Christ, i.e., you are named from Christ, Christians: so that at Antioch the disciples were first named Christians (Acts 11:21).
